missed_changes: Turned optimistic on US regulation rather than reaffirming hostility — publicly celebrated David Sacks's crypto-czar appointment ('Excellent choice')
Strong new emphasis on cross-chain interoperability / bridges (LayerZero, Espresso) not anticipated in the prediction
New emphasis on privacy-preserving ZK computation (Aleo mainnet) and confidentiality
New emphasis on restaking infrastructure (Eigen × Infura)
2025 prediction that formal verification will become increasingly important
Interest in L3s and composability as recurring themes
notes: His later statements are almost entirely deal/ecosystem celebrations (a16z portfolio: Daylight, XMTP, Aleo, LayerZero, Espresso, Eigen, Murmur) rather than restatements of his macro thesis. DePIN is clearly confirmed via Daylight (decentralized energy grid). Identity and stablecoins/payments are weakly confirmed via Aleo's identity systems and the LayerZero×PayPal partnership respectively. The headline 'AI x crypto' thesis is notably absent from the window. None of his three concerns reappear, and his regulatory stance appears to have flipped from hostility-as-headwind to optimism. ETH/SOL token-level views go unaddressed. Overall tone (frequent '🔥', State of Crypto exponential-growth framing) is consistently risk-on.
